title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 · Contract Defense
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for node 24, typescript 7, and aws sdk for javascript v3 and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for AWS SDK v3 TypeScript client contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Construct S3Client from immutable process configuration, short-lived credential and region providers, one owned request handler, and explicit shutdown and test lifetimes.
Treat command inputs, optional output fields, enums, paginators, metadata, and streaming bodies as SDK transport shapes that still require runtime and business validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9892 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce pinned Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, modular client, runtime validation, and service-error narrowing; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Catch unknown, preserve cause, distinguish AbortError, narrow S3ServiceException and named service cases, and retain $metadata request evidence without string-matching messages.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Service Exception Narrowing.
